title: Emergency Numbers Every Tourist Must Know in China
metaDescription: Essential emergency numbers for tourists visiting China. Save these contacts for police, medical, and fire emergencies before your trip.

sections.heading: Essential Emergency Numbers to Save Now
sections.content: China uses three main emergency numbers: dial 110 for police, 119 for fire department, and 120 for medical emergencies and ambulance services. These numbers work nationwide from any phone, free of charge, and operators typically speak basic English in major cities like Beijing and Shanghai.

sections.heading: Medical and Travel Assistance Hotlines
sections.content: For non-emergency health advice, call 12320. If you need tourist assistance, dial 12301. Lost your passport? Contact your embassy immediately. For language barriers, download a translation app beforehand. Major hospitals in tourist areas often have English-speaking staff at international departments.

sections.heading: Smart Tips for Staying Safe
sections.content: Save your hotel address in Chinese characters on your phone. Keep your embassy contact information handy. Download offline maps before exploring. Share your daily itinerary with someone you trust. Most public phones can dial emergency numbers for free, even without a phone card.

keyTakeaways: Memorize 110 for police, 119 for fire, and 120 for ambulance
Save your hotel address written in Chinese characters
Download translation and offline map apps before arriving
relatedPhrases: how to call emergency services in China
